results.caption:
They didn't just build ransomware. They built a BRAND.

DragonForce launched on BreachForums December 6, 2023 — not as a tool, but as a cartel with a logo, a revenue model, and a recruitment pitch.

80% to affiliates. 20% to the house.
363 victims. 30+ countries. £300M+ in damage.

This is ransomware-as-a-service operating like a Fortune 500 franchise.

The dragon isn't mythology. It's a business model.

#Cybersecurity #Ransomware #DragonForce #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: A mythic dragon forged from ransomware code stops LinkedIn scrollers cold — brand identity as threat actor propaganda made visceral.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
The ransom note arrived before IT even knew the network was down.

DragonForce's ChaCha8 encryption hit Windows, Linux, AND ESXi simultaneously.
First they killed the EDR — using truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys.
Then they encrypted everything.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods.
Three British retail giants. One summer. £300M+ in impact.

The note wasn't the end of the attack.
It was the opening negotiation.

This is what BYOVD looks like when it lands in a boardroom.

#Ransomware #DragonForce #CyberAttack #IncidentResponse #BYOVD
results.hook: A ransomware note splayed across a shattered boardroom screen makes the abstract financial horror of £300M devastatingly concrete.

results.caption:
No confirmed nation-state. No arrested operators. No verified faces.

DragonForce emerged from the dark on December 6, 2023 — and attribution remains officially UNKNOWN.

Malaysia-linked forum activity. Pro-Palestinian messaging in early samples. Scattered Spider collaboration for English-language vishing. RansomHub absorbed April 8, 2025.

Is it a hacktivist collective turned cartel?
A state-sponsored proxy with plausible deniability?
Or just very sophisticated independent criminals?

The most dangerous threat actors are the ones you can't pin to a map.

#ThreatIntelligence #DragonForce #AttributionAnalysis #Cybersecurity #NationState
results.hook: A shadowed figure behind a fragmented map of competing attribution theories forces the unsettling truth — we still don't know who built DragonForce.
